zh.lifeSummary: 當Joane Elizabeth Collett於1545年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾武坡 斯勞特時，她的父親Henry John Collett25歲，母親Joan Hanks25歲。她於1570年4月10日與John Fenne在英國英格蘭韋斯特 素可阿盧德爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1604年12月14日享年59歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Collett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas . There was also a feminine form, Collette, which may also have given rise to a surname. The name could also mean ‘Coll the younger’.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

nameMeanings.name: Joane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Joane Elizabeth Collett was born in 1545, in Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Henry John Collett, was 25 and her mother, Joan Hanks, was 25. She married John Fenne on 10 April 1570, in Arundel, West Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 14 December 1604, at the age of 59, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

Elle a épousé John Fenne le 10 avril 1570, à Arundel, West Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 décembre 1604, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joane Elizabeth Collett è nata il nel 1545 nel Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Henry John Collett, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Joan Hanks, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato John Fenne il 10 aprile 1570, nel Arundel, West Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 14 dicembre 1604, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolta a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joane Elizabeth Collettが1545年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアウップエル スラウターで生まれた時，父Henry John Collettは25歳，母Joan Hanksは25歳であった。1570年4月10日にイギリスイギリスウエスト スセックスアルンデルでJohn Fenneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1604年12月14日に59歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Peter's Church, Upper Slaughter, C: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 업퍼 슬로그h터 스트 피터'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Joane Elizabeth Collett(이)가 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 업퍼 슬로그h터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry John Collett(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Hanks(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1570년 4월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 수세스 아런들에서 John Fenne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1604년 12월 14일 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joane Elizabeth Collett nasceu em 1545, em Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry John Collett, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan Hanks, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com John Fenne em 10 de abril de 1570, em Arundel, West Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de dezembro de 1604, com 59 anos, e foi sepultada em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.Saint Peter's Church, Upper Slaughter, C: ст питир'с чурч, уппир слотир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joane Elizabeth Collett родилась в 1545 г. в местности уппир слотир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Henry John Collett было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Hanks, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Fenne 10 апреля 1570 г. в местности арундель, вест суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 14 декабря 1604 г. в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joane Elizabeth Collett nació en 1545, en Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry John Collett, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan Hanks, tenía 25 años. Se casó con John Fenne el 10 de abril de 1570, en Arundel, West Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 14 de diciembre de 1604, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultada en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
